instruction:
Write a complete testthat test file for `rsample::initial_time_split`.

Package: rsample v1.3.2.9000
Description: General Resampling Infrastructure
Function: initial_time_split
Parameters: data, prop, lag
Test patterns used: expect_snapshot, test_that
Expectations used: expect_identical, expect_s3_class, expect_snapshot

Function Code:
```r
initial_time_split <- function(
  data,
  prop = 3 / 4,
  lag = lifecycle::deprecated(),
  ...
) {
  check_dots_empty()
  check_prop(prop)

  if (lifecycle::is_present(lag)) {
    lifecycle::deprecate_soft(
      when = "1.3.1.9000",
      what = "initial_time_split(lag)",
      details = "Please lag your predictors prior to splitting the dataset."
    )
  } else {
    lag <- 0
  }

  if (!is.numeric(lag) | !(lag %% 1 == 0)) {
    cli_abort("{.arg lag} must be a whole number.")
  }

  n_train <- floor(nrow(data) * prop)

  if (lag > n_train) {
    cli_abort(
      "{.arg lag} must be less than or equal to the number of training observations."
    )
  }

  split <- rsplit(data, 1:n_train, (n_train + 1 - lag):nrow(data))
  splits <- list(split)
  ids <- "Resample1"
  rset <- new_rset(splits, ids)

  res <- rset$splits[[1]]

  attrib <- list(
    prop = prop,
    lag = lag
  )
  for (i in names(attrib)) {
    attr(res, i) <- attrib[[i]]
  }

  class(res) <- c("initial_time_split", "initial_split", class(res))
  res
}
```

Requirements:
1. Use test_that() blocks with descriptive names that reference `initial_time_split`
2. Test basic functionality using appropriate expectations (expect_identical, expect_s3_class, expect_snapshot)
3. Test edge cases: NULL input, empty strings, missing arguments
4. Test error conditions with expect_error()
5. Each test_that() block should test one logical behavior
